name: socratic-codex
description: Goal-lifecycle control for Codex: bind intent to a persisted goal contract, gate risky changes, restore the contract after compaction, and block unverified completion claims with behavioral evidence checks.

interface.longDescription: Socratic Codex protects user-owned intent across discovery, goal binding, planning, execution steering, diagnostic recovery, boundary gates, and acceptance closure. It persists the goal contract to .socratic/contract.md, restores it after compaction or resume, re-anchors on brake or drift signals, asks only action-changing questions, and blocks completion claims until behavioral evidence (verification commands run, or a fresh Verification record in the contract) matches done criteria. Use it for /goal drafting and steering, solution-shaped requests, risky target changes, stuck diagnostics, and acceptance handoff; skip routine deterministic work outside an active lifecycle.
